Proposition 5.2.9. Relation between sets of content \(0\) and sets of measure \(0\).

  1. A set of content \(0\) must be a set of measure \(0\text{.}\)
  2. The closure of a set of content \(0\) must be a set of content \(0\text{.}\)
  3. A countable union of sets of content \(0\) must be a set of measure \(0\text{.}\) As a consequence, a countable union of sets of content \(0\) must be a set of measure \(0\text{.}\)
  4. A compact set of measure \(0\) is also a set of content \(0\text{.}\)

Proof.

in-context
